8:
Such people tend toward self-centeredness; the troubles of others rarely concern them. They keep a certain distance in relationships and with people in general. They are capable of love, yet their circle of close ones is very limited. They take failures hard and fear difficulties. A sense of duty is present, but how much a person draws upon it depends on the individual and on their upbringing.
These people are impatient, restless, and dislike routine. They can be somewhat infantile (behaving like children, prone to whims). They tend to deny their more difficult traits and do not take criticism well — they find it hard and painful to accept, especially from those close to them.
They blame the people around them and outside circumstances for their problems. Their sense of duty is weak and needs to be strengthened. Their inner boundaries are strong. When making decisions, such a person relies solely on their own opinion, disregarding others. People without an "8" (children in particular) need to be taught a sense of responsibility, family traditions, and duty by example. Most often they are freedom-loving by nature. They are impatient and will not tolerate pressure or being told what to do. They are inclined to blame others and reluctant to take on too much themselves.
They can be irresponsible and may let others down; the word "must" does not resonate with them (often this trait shifts after having children, through strict upbringing, or simply with maturity). When the "8" is absent, people may act without conscience — yet they are not greedy and know how to express gratitude. In a past life they made mistakes, deceived someone, or failed to return what they owed. For this reason, money does not come easily to them in this life. When the "8" is present, these are happy and fortunate people. In a past life they were responsible and honorable, and in this life money is given to them as a bonus.
Such children must, from an early age, be taught independence and encouraged toward tolerance and responsibility. Get them a pet and assign them a clear area of responsibility in household chores (washing dishes, taking out the trash).
Such people are lucky without much effort. They may receive gifts of fate, and a great deal slips past them, including a healthy streak of adventurousness. This is a bonus for merits earned in a past life. They hold an inner right to ask fate for things and receive them without much strain. They have the ability to bring to life the thoughts and wishes they speak aloud. They possess an instinctive knowledge and understanding of the laws of the universe. They sense the presence of the Higher Powers and a connection with God.
88:
A sense of duty is given from birth. One might call them "the kind-hearted ones." Kindness becomes the norm of their behavior; they are very tolerant, mindful of their loved ones, and people of duty — helping others becomes a necessity for them. Anyone may take advantage of them.
These people are seekers of truth; they always sense a lie and refrain from pointing it out to their companion only out of tolerance or pity. This is the Lawyer's Code. A sense of responsibility and rightness is strongly developed — the mark of a lawyer; they make good family people. It is a sign of success and the fulfillment of wishes. Their sense of rightness and critical judgment can be carried to the point of fanaticism. Difficulties in building relationships with the opposite sex and with other people, owing to a fierce sense of rightness, ARE POSSIBLE.
These people are very caring, though they may slip into overprotectiveness and smother others with their care. They can love a great many people and know how to share. 88 and 888 are the golden mean when it comes to luck. The person is fortunate, but must share. These people dislike it when others touch their belongings. They dislike going to doctors — and may pay a price for it. Their sense of duty and critical judgment gives rise to difficulties in relationships with the opposite sex and with other people. The Lawyer's Code: responsible people one can always rely on, who often take all initiative upon themselves ("I'll do it all myself") and do not know how to delegate. They are controllers, overseeing everything within their area of responsibility. Honest and decent. They always blame themselves for problems rather than circumstances, and it is easy to instill a sense of guilt in them. When 3, 4, and 8 are present, these people are dissatisfied with life. Physically healthy, yet they often pretend otherwise to get what they want.
888:
A sense of duty is given from birth. One might call them "the kind-hearted ones." Kindness becomes the norm of their behavior; they are very tolerant, mindful of their loved ones, and people of duty — helping others becomes a necessity for them. Anyone may take advantage of them.
These people are seekers of truth; they always sense a lie and refrain from pointing it out to their companion only out of tolerance or pity. This is the Lawyer's Code. These people are very caring, though they may slip into overprotectiveness and smother others with their care. They can love a great many people and know how to share. They make good family people. The tripled sign
of Infinity, "888" (the great number), is a powerful sign of luck and the fulfillment of wishes. Their sense of duty is so strong that they feel compelled to share and to help. So too with care: in a woman, the caring impulse is so strong that she does not notice how she controls those around her — relatives, children, her spouse. There is self-sacrifice for the sake of something.
Responsible people one can always rely on, who often take all initiative upon themselves ("I'll do it all myself") and do not know how to delegate. They are controllers, overseeing everything within their area of responsibility. Their sense of duty and critical judgment gives rise to difficulties in relationships with the opposite sex and with other people.
They are talismans for others: when they enter into a bond with someone (marriage, friendship), they bring that person luck and success.
They are talismans for others: when they enter into a bond with someone (marriage, friendship), they bring that person luck and success. They can be fanatical, often losing themselves in work and forgetting their family, or the reverse — losing themselves in family and forgetting themselves. For such people the world is often divided into black and white; they can be very categorical and even stubborn in their judgments, tiresome, with a strong sense of duty, exerting pressure on those around them, critical and fastidious.
8888:
An overload of the trait, where all the qualities inherent to 88 and 888 may intensify greatly, or, conversely, fail to manifest at all.
If such a person lives in sheltered, pampered conditions (dependent, irresponsible), it may show up as a sense of duty being absent — like "no 8" or a single "8."
нет:
Such people tend toward self-centeredness; the troubles of others rarely concern them. They keep a certain distance in relationships and with people in general. They are capable of love, yet their circle of close ones is very limited. They take failures hard and fear difficulties. A sense of duty is present, but how much a person draws upon it depends on the individual and on their upbringing.
These people are impatient, restless, and dislike routine. They can be somewhat infantile (behaving like children, prone to whims). They tend to deny their more difficult traits and do not take criticism well — they find it hard and painful to accept, especially from those close to them.
They blame the people around them and outside circumstances for their problems. Their sense of duty is weak and needs to be strengthened. Their inner boundaries are strong. When making decisions, such a person relies solely on their own opinion, disregarding others. People without an "8" (children in particular) need to be taught a sense of responsibility, family traditions, and duty by example. Most often they are freedom-loving by nature. They are impatient and will not tolerate pressure or being told what to do. They are inclined to blame others and reluctant to take on too much themselves.
They can be irresponsible and may let others down; the word "must" does not resonate with them (often this trait shifts after having children, through strict upbringing, or simply with maturity). When the "8" is absent, people may act without conscience — yet they are not greedy and know how to express gratitude. In a past life they made mistakes, deceived someone, or failed to return what they owed. For this reason, money does not come easily to them in this life. When the "8" is present, these are happy and fortunate people. In a past life they were responsible and honorable, and in this life money is given to them as a bonus.
Such children must, from an early age, be taught independence and encouraged toward tolerance and responsibility. Get them a pet and assign them a clear area of responsibility in household chores (washing dishes, taking out the trash).
Such people have to earn everything in life through their own labor. They do not feel a connection "from above," tend to compare who received more and who received less, and gifts of fate are few or none at all.
The theme of Life Purpose concerns them little or not at all. Everything given to them in life is given without conditions. If they search for their place in life, it is not driven by the theme of Life Purpose but by other motives. They may look for an occupation they enjoy, for material stability, good social standing, or the satisfaction of other inner needs. They have no feeling of having promised anything to anyone from above. They are entitled to define for themselves the reasons why they wish to pursue this or that endeavor. But to achieve results, they will have to work hard. Their psychological challenge is the feeling of injustice.
